business.headline: Silver jumps Rs 5,000, gold up Rs 2,000: Time to buy or sell?Silver and gold prices have surged sharply today, sparking fresh interest among investors after recent volatility. So is this rally a buying opportunity or time to book profits?

Sensex closes 291 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; RIL up 1%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 291.17 points, or 0.38%, to close at 77,094.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 89.80 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 24,102.90.

India's economy is booming. So why are foreign investors looking away?For years, India rolled out the red carpet for foreign investors, citing its vast market, consumer base and ease of doing business. Now, as global capital chases artificial intelligence and semiconductors, those strengths no longer seem enough.

8th Pay Commission: What pensioners and family pension beneficiaries can expectThe 8th Pay Commission has begun consultations in Lucknow with unions, associations and department representatives. Its recommendations could shape pensions, family pensions and retirement security for millions.

Can Calcutta Stock Exchange make a comeback? Bengal govt proposes revival planAfter years of uncertainty and legal hurdles, the Calcutta Stock Exchange has received a fresh lifeline from the Bengal government. Can the revival proposal help bring trading back to the exchange?

India wants lower tariffs in US trade deal; hopes to sign 1st phase before July 24India is pressing the United States for lower tariffs and better market access in the proposed trade pact. The talks also carry wider significance as both sides weigh sensitive sectors and broader strategic cooperation.

Old vs new tax regime: How many times can you change your choice?Old or new tax regime? It's a choice many taxpayers revisit every year. But before making the switch, it's important to know how often you're actually allowed to change your tax regime.

Will Jio IPO really unlock big gains for Reliance investors?Mukesh Ambani has confirmed Jio Platforms' IPO filing at Reliance's AGM. Analysts say the listing may improve transparency but not automatically spark a sharp Reliance rerating.

Could you get an income tax notice by June 30? Don't panic, here's how to respondJune 30 may not be on every taxpayer's radar, but it is an important date for those who have already filed their returns. Here's who could receive a scrutiny notice and how to respond if one arrives.

Reliance shares gain over 2%: Why is RIL stock rising today?Reliance Industries shares rose after Jio Platforms filed its draft papers for an IPO. The move sharpened focus on value unlocking and the group's next growth engines.

Can Jio become India's biggest IPO ever? Here's how it compares to top 5 IPOsIf current estimates hold true, the JIO IPO could raise as much as $4 billion, or around Rs 37,000 crore. That would comfortably surpass Hyundai Motor India's Rs 27,859 crore IPO, which currently holds the record as the largest public issue in Indian history.

NSE vs Jio IPO: How do the two giants compare?NSE has filed its DRHP, while Jio Platforms is set to file its prospectus with Sebi. The twin moves bring India's most-awaited IPOs closer and spotlight financialisation and digitalisation.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; IT stocks gainThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 404.20 points, or 0.53%, to 77,207.10 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 117.25 points, or 0.49%, to 24,130.35.

The taxman is using AI. These ITR mistakes could get flaggedTax experts have listed eight common ITR filing mistakes that can draw the department's attention. With AI-led data matching expanding, even small mismatches can delay refunds or trigger notices.

Why RBI drew the line on mis-selling by banksThe RBI has issued its strongest framework yet against mis-selling by banks and regulated entities. The regulations target dark patterns, forced bundling and unsuitable sales while shifting accountability to institutions.

Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningAccenture reported strong AI demand but trimmed its growth outlook, signalling that clients are still holding back on spending. The warning hammered Indian IT stocks, with Infosys leading the rout.

entertainment.headline: Om Puri's first wife Seema Kapoor recalls his affair, losing unborn child Writer and filmmaker Seema Kapoor recalled how Om Puri's affair shattered their marriage while she was pregnant. She said the loss of her child, divorce papers and her decision to avoid a legal fight made the period harder.

Batwara 1947: Did you know Sunny Deol film is based on a play banned in Pakistan? Amid the curiosity around Sunny Deol's upcoming film Batwara 1947, not many would know that it is based on a play that was reportedly banned in Pakistan.

Vijay's first question after oath was for his father, says CM's mother Shoba Vijay's mother looked back at the emotional moment that followed his swearing-in as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and the long journey that led there. The actor-politician is celebrating his 52nd birthday on June 22.

What if it was your daughter? Orry challenges idea of gender-neutral bathrooms A podcast exchange between Orry and content creator Kavya Karnatac on gender-neutral bathrooms has gone viral. The clip has fuelled an online debate.

House of the Dragon 3: The shocking incest twist that leaves even GoT fans stunned A shocking mother-son kiss in the House of the Dragon Season 3 premiere has left viewers stunned. Here's why the controversial twist is dominating discussions and what it means for the Targaryen dynasty.
Top 7 classic Indian TV shows available on OTT From Shaktimaan and Shaka Laka Boom Boom to Sarabhai vs Sarabhai and Malgudi Days, several iconic Indian TV shows are now available on OTT platforms.

In pictures: The 10 scenes that defined House of the Dragon's season 3 premiere House of the Dragon returns with a dramatic premiere featuring Aegon II's escape, Alicent's failed scheme, a shocking confrontation with Aemond and the devastating Battle of the Gullet that changes the course of the war.

Vijay turns 52: Wishes pour in from PM Modi, Rahul Gandhi, MK Stalin, Kamal Haasan Vijay's birthday drew messages from political leaders, film personalities and fans across social media. The celebrations also renewed focus on Jana Nayagan, which is still awaiting final CBFC certification.

Vijay's mother Shoba recalls his son being upset during peak election campaign In a recent television interview, Vijay's mother, Shoba Chandrasekhar, recalled how he struggled through criticism and a bruising political campaign. Her remarks renewed focus on the difficult path that took him from cinema to the chief minister's office.

Venkatesh, Kalyan Ram film promo has an Animal connection. Watch video Director Anil Ravipudi unveiled a comic concept video for his upcoming film with Venkatesh and Kalyan Ram. Featuring an Animal-inspired spoof, the promo reveals the film's Rayalaseema backdrop and family-entertainer setting.

At 52, Thalapathy Vijay remains one of Indian cinema's ultimate aura king. 7 proofs As Thalapathy Vijay turns 52, we revisit seven moments from his recent films that perfectly capture his unmatched swagger, screen presence and effortless ability to turn simple scenes into fan-favourite mass moments.

Actor Indresh Malik on his role in TVF's The Pyramid Scheme: Character arc felt new Actor Indresh Malik said the character arc of Divya Jyoti in TVF's The Pyramid Scheme felt new and exciting, drawing him to the project. The actor also reflected on working in a grounded, middle-class setting and bringing different shades to the complex role.

Mahabharat's Shafaq Naaz introduces husband after secret 2024 wedding Actor Shafaq Naaz has announced her marriage to Zeeshan through an Instagram post. The reveal surprised fans and television colleagues, who sent warm wishes after months of privacy.

Tom Holland recalls Robert Downey Jr's surprise Avengers: Doomsday call Tom Holland shared that Robert Downey Jr personally called him to reveal his Avengers: Doomsday casting as Doctor Doom. The exchange highlights their ongoing bond, built during their time co-starring in the Avengers films and Spider-Man: Homecoming.

Bride-to-be Taylor Swift jets out of Rhode Island as fireworks light up the night Pop sensation Taylor Swift was seen departing near her Rhode Island estate as a late-night fireworks display fuelled speculation about possible pre-wedding festivities. Here's what has been reported so far.

Kerala Assembly pays homage to National Award-winning Malayalam actor Salim Kumar The Kerala Legislative Assembly paid homage to Malayalam actor Salim Kumar, who died on June 6. Members remembered his contributions to Malayalam cinema and observed a minute's silence in his honour.

Sunil Pal takes dig at Samay Raina's 'brush' joke: There's filth in your mouth Sunil Pal answered Samay Raina's repeated brush joke through an Instagram reel with mimic Jay Verma. The latest response extends their long-running feud across stand-up, streaming shows and social media.

Explained: Why actor Shweta Menon quit Malayalam film body amid growing crisis Actor Shweta Menon resigned as AMMA president after a controversy escalated over the annual meeting regarding accounts and control. The exit of the full executive committee has pushed the association into yet another crisis.

Sona Mohapatra says Bollywood gives heartbreak songs to men, cites Arijit's Zaalima Singer Sona Mohapatra has criticised Bollywood's music industry, claiming that heartbreak and romantic songs are largely reserved for male singers. Citing Arijit Singh's Zaalima as an example, she questioned why female voices are often sidelined even in duets.

general news.headline: 15 killed in massive fire at Lucknow building housing pet shop, animation studio Live updatesA pet shop, an animation centre and a building engulfed in flames. The Lucknow fire triggered a massive rescue effort before ending in tragedy for several lives.

6 rebel Uddhav Sena MPs join Eknath Shinde's faction after days of speculation  Live updatesThe induction of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs into Eknath Shinde's camp is being viewed as a major political gain for the ruling faction and a setback for Uddhav Thackeray.

Students waited over a month for NEET re-test. Couldn't they be spared 2 minutes?The NEET re-test gave the examination system a second chance after the paper leak controversy. But for some students, reaching minutes late because of rain, traffic, accidents or document confusion meant there was no second chance at all.

Who is Andy Burnham, King of the North, frontrunner for UK PM's post?Keir Starmer's resignation as British Prime Minister and Labour Party leader has triggered a search for his successor. Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham, who won a parliamentary by-election last week, is being seen as the frontrunner. Who's Burnham, and why is he known as the "King of the North"?

Iran's Switzerland snub of JD Vance, and Washington's humiliation is completeIranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi briefly walked out of the opening session of US-Iran talks in Switzerland after speaking to Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif. The episode and later shifts in Washington's stance sharpened the view that Tehran was negotiating from a position of strength.

Mamata's TMC to Uddhav Sena: Are rebels sugarcoating party splits now?From Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress to Uddhav Thackeray's Shiv Sena (UBT), recent defections to the BJP-led NDA have unfolded with an unusual lack of public hostility. Both the rebels and the leaders refrained from direct attacks on one another. Is India witnessing a new, sugar-coated style of political defection?

UAE seeks to buy BrahMos, Akashteer to boost post-Middle East war defenceAs the UAE moves to bolster its defence capabilities following the Iran war, it has initiated early-stage talks with India to acquire the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile and the Akashteer air defence system.

Keir Starmer quits as UK PM, sixth leader in 10 years to step downPrime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation after months of pressure from Labour lawmakers, paving the way for a leadership contest as the United Kingdom prepares for its seventh leader in a little over a decade.

Has Trump succeeded in making Vance the face of America's humiliation by Iran?US President Donald Trump on multiple occasions, albeit in jest, suggested that if the Iran talks failed, he would blame JD Vance for it. Trump sent Vance for negotiations to Geneva, and the developments suggest that the Vice President might actually be emerging as the face of America's humiliation by Iran.

Live: CM Yogi Adityanath cuts short Aligarh visit after fire tragedy in LucknowLucknow Fire Live Updates: At least 14 people, including children, have died in a massive fire that broke out at a commercial building in Lucknow's Aliganj area on Monday. The blaze prompted a large-scale rescue operation. Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has directed senior officials to rush to the spot and review the rescue and relief operations. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates about the fire.

Bihar on the boil over encounter killing of man who made anti-government postsBihar government has ordered a judicial inquiry into the Bhojpur encounter in which Bharat Bhushan Tiwari was killed. The move follows protests, questions over the police action and concern within the BJP.

Munnabhai MBBS played out at a Bihar NEET re-test centre, 24 arrestedBihar Police have busted an alleged proxy exam racket during the NEET-UG retest in Lakhisarai. The case has raised fresh questions about insider access, student involvement and exam safeguards.

Satellite captures Godzilla El Nino wave rising like never beforeA Nasa satellite has tracked unusually high sea levels across the equatorial Pacific as a powerful El Nino gathers strength. The readings point to a deep store of heat moving eastward, raising the risk of major weather disruption worldwide.

A message to selectors? Sooryavanshi's post-match chat after 29-ball 94 goes viralIndia A vs Sri Lanka A: Following a record-shattering 29-ball 94 against Sri Lanka A, teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has sent a clear message to senior ODI selectors, reminding his followers of his extensive 50-over pedigree in a viral post-match interview.

Video: Man jumps from first floor window to escape huge Lucknow building fireA massive fire broke out at a commercial building in Lucknow, which killed 15 people. Dramatic visuals captured a man leaping from a first-floor window to escape the flames and dense smoke that engulfed the building.

Why are homeowners turning to thermocol wall technology for cooling?As temperatures continue to rise, EPS wall technology is drawing interest as a cooling solution for homes. Made with thermocol-based panels, the construction method aims to reduce heat transfer, improve insulation and lower energy use.

1 extra minute at lunch, 60 minutes of unpaid work? Viral notice says 'eat faster'A viral office notice warning employees that every extra minute spent at lunch could result in an hour of unpaid work has triggered backlash online. The 'eat faster' memo sparked criticism, jokes and renewed debate over workplace culture, overtime and employee rights.

Cancer served on a plate? How chemicals banned in Europe are killing IndiansCarcinogenic chemicals, which get Indian agricultural exports rejected in Europe, continue to be sprayed at Indian farms without checks even though they are banned abroad. Why does the government allow these chemicals to find a place on Indian farms and then reach plates?

India's economy is booming. So why are foreign investors looking away?For years, India rolled out the red carpet for foreign investors, citing its vast market, consumer base and ease of doing business. Now, as global capital chases artificial intelligence and semiconductors, those strengths no longer seem enough.

Rs 3,000 for jobless graduates, free bus rides for women in BJP's Bengal budgetWest Bengal Finance Minister Swapan Dasgupta announced the Bharosa Scheme for unemployed people aged 21 to 45 years. Under the scheme, graduates not having a job will be entitled to financial assistance of 3,000 per month. Meanwhile, unemployed non-graduates will receive 2,000 per month, the minister said.

A Vijay for every generation: How Thalapathy rebuilt himself into Jana NayaganAs Vijay turns 52, his 33-year screen journey is traced through three distinct fan eras. From the romantic hero of the 90s to the Chief Minister of today, Vijay didn't just age with his audience, he rebuilt himself for each one.

Not political tools: BJP vs Congress after students miss NEET re-test in BengaluruThe NEET-UG re-test in Bengaluru triggered a political clash after some late candidates were denied entry amid traffic complaints. The BJP blamed the Congress convention at Palace Grounds, while the government called the charge manufactured outrage.

Will go to war: Pakistan's Khawaja Asif threatens India over Indus Waters TreatyPakistan's Defence Minister said Islamabad could go to war if India threatens its access to Indus waters. The warning sharpens tensions over the suspended treaty as Pakistan raises alarm over water security.

Iran walked out, JD Vance watched, Pakistanis stunned as cameras kept rollingThe Iranian delegation eventually returned to the negotiating table. But by then, a brief walkout, a surprised Pakistani delegation and a watchful JD Vance had already become the defining image of the day.

sports.headline: Ronaldo hat-trick against Uzbekistan? Michael Owen slams critics of Portugal star FIFA World Cup 2026: Michael Owen defended Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's draw with DR Congo and backed him to answer critics against Uzbekistan. His remarks come amid scrutiny over Ronaldo's form, Portugal's balance and Roberto Martínez's authority.

I'm a failure as captain: Chamari Athapaththu after Sri Lanka face early exit threat Chamari Athapaththu was left emotional as Sri Lanka suffered a five-wicket defeat to the West Indies, leaving their Women's T20 World Cup campaign on the brink of an early exit. Athapaththu said she felt like a 'failure as captain' for not guiding Sri Lanka to a World Cup semi.

Motherhood and cricket can coexist, says ICC in landmark move The ICC has launched its Return to Play Post-Pregnancy Guidelines to support women cricketers returning to elite sport after childbirth through a structured, science-backed framework.

Mo Salah joins wild street party in Vancouver after Egypt's historic victory Mohamed Salah joined Egypt fans on Vancouver's streets after the 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand. The celebrations marked Egypt's first FIFA World Cup finals victory and the release of a 92-year wait.

How Egypt coach's pep talk inspired historic FIFA World Cup win vs New Zealand FIFA World Cup: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan revealed the stirring speech he delivered that helped Egypt recover from a 0-1 deficit to beat New Zealand 3-1 in the Group G match.

Constant stoppages make World Cup matches tougher: Scaloni on hydration breaks Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni has questioned FIFA's newly introduced hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, saying the stoppages disrupt the natural flow of matches and can alter tactical dynamics during games.

Messi, Mbappe and Haaland return to action, chasing more history FIFA World Cup 2026: As football's ultimate heavyweights return for their second group fixtures, Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland look to consolidate opening-day masterclasses and steer their nations toward early knockout qualification in North America.

Are Harmanpreet Kaur's India in danger of getting knocked out early? Women's T20 World Cup 2026: India's defeat to South Africa has turned their Group A campaign into a qualification battle. The result leaves Harmanpreet Kaur's side needing wins over Bangladesh and Australia to stay in semi-final contention.

ICC punishes England with 12-point WTC penalty after loss to New Zealand England's World Test Championship (WTC) hopes suffered a major setback after the International Cricket Council (ICC) docked the team 12 points for a slow over-rate during their 253-run defeat to New Zealand in the second Test at The Oval.

A message to selectors? Sooryavanshi's post-match chat after 29-ball 94 goes viral India A vs Sri Lanka A: Following a record-shattering 29-ball 94 against Sri Lanka A, teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has sent a clear message to senior ODI selectors, reminding his followers of his extensive 50-over pedigree in a viral post-match interview.

Iran leave handwritten note in Los Angeles locker room and it has a plea for peace FIFA World Cup: Iran's players left an emotional handwritten message in their SoFi Stadium locker room on Sunday, thanking Los Angeles for its hospitality and supporters for standing by the team during a challenging World Cup campaign.

Iran played beautiful game under worst conditions: Coach Amir hails Belgium drawIran coach Amir Ghalenoei hailed his side's performance after a hard-fought 0-0 draw against Belgium in the FIFA World Cup, saying his players produced a "beautiful game" despite battling what he described as the "worst possible conditions" heading into the tournament.

Ignore the numbers? India back clutch Jemimah Rodrigues to defy the critics againWomen's T20 World Cup: Amol Muzumdar has given his unflinching backing to the out-of-form Jemimah Rodrigues, labelling her a clutch match-winner as India face a crucial World Cup tightrope following defeat to South Africa.

Egypt end 92-year wait for win at FIFA World Cup as Salah buries New ZealandMohamed Salah inspired Egypt to a historic 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand on Sunday as the Pharaohs ended a 92-year wait for their first FIFA World Cup victory. Goals from Mostafa Ziko, Salah and Mahmoud Trezeguet secured the landmark result and sent Egypt to the top of Group G.

Cape Verde eye World Cup knockout berth after battling Uruguay draw: Coach BubistaCape Verde coach Bubista believes his side have earned the right to target a place in the World Cup knockout rounds after battling to a 2-2 draw with Uruguay, a result that kept the debutants firmly in contention for qualification from Group H.

After watching 2022 World Cup from a classroom, Yamal revels in dream debut goalLamine Yamal marked his first World Cup start with a goal as Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0. The strike completed a leap from watching the 2022 tournament in a classroom to shaping Spain's campaign on the pitch.

Vozinha's mum watches on as Cape Verde continue captivating journeyCape Verde's remarkable World Cup story continued on Sunday as they held Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in Miami, with goalkeeper Vozinha's mother, Evora, watching from the stands. After missing her son's historic performance against Spain due to visa issues, Evora was finally able to attend following a successful fundraising effort.

1st WC goal, another point: Cape Verde's fairytale rolls on with Uruguay drawCape Verde's fairytale got a new chapter as the African side scored their first World Cup goal and secured another point with a 2-2 draw against Uruguay.

Who is Alireza Beiranvand? Iran's wall with two Guinness records stuns BelgiumFIFA World Cup 2026: Alireza Beiranvand added his name to the growing list of goalkeeper heroes at FIFA World Cup 2026 with a seven-save masterclass against Belgium. The Iranian shot-stopper, who holds two Guinness World Records, frustrated the Red Devils and kept Iran's knockout hopes alive with a crucial clean sheet.

NZ 1-3 EGY HIGHLIGHTS: Salah inspires Egypt's historic winNew Zealand (NZ) vs Egypt (EGY) HIGHLIGHTS, FIFA World Cup 2026: Egypt's wait for a World Cup victory is finally over. Mohamed Salah was once again the man for the big occasion as the Pharaohs beat New Zealand in a gripping Group G contest, before producing a determined defensive display to see out the closing stages. It is a result that will be celebrated across Egypt, with the national team securing its first-ever win on football's biggest stage.

The Beiranvand wall helps Iran secure vital point against 10-man BelgiumIran held 10-man Belgium to a goalless draw in Los Angeles with Alireza Beiranvand making a series of crucial saves. The point strengthened Iran's knockout hopes and exposed Belgium's wastefulness despite their control of the ball.

Serena Williams set for return to Wimbledon as singles wildcardSerena Williams' remarkable tennis comeback has taken another dramatic turn after the seven-time champion received a Wimbledon singles wildcard. Four years after her last singles appearance at the All England Club, the 23-time Grand Slam winner is set to return to Centre Court once again.

Kohli, Karan Aujla groove on Winning Speech, says song reminds him of his dadVirat Kohli joined Karan Aujla on stage at the One8 event in New Delhi as the singer performed Winning Speech. Kohli later said the song resonates deeply and explain how it reminds him of his father.

Harmanpreet Kaur laments Marizanne Kapp's drop chances after India's costly defeatWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: Marizanne Kapp made India pay for three dropped catches as South Africa beat the reigning ODI world champions by six wickets on Sunday in Manchester.

Lamine Yamal steals the show as Spain roar back to life with Saudi Arabia routFIFA World Cup 2026: Lamine Yamal inspired Spain's 4-0 World Cup win over Saudi Arabia in Atlanta. The result dispelled doubts after the Cape Verde draw and sets up a sterner test against Uruguay.

Sloppy India lose Manchester thriller to South Africa, in danger of early exitWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: Marizanne Kapp scored an unbeaten 81 as South Africa defeated India by six wickets on Sunday in Manchester. With the defeat, India's upcoming match against Australia now becomes a crucial fixture in the race to secure a place in the semi-finals.

BEL 0-0 IRN Highlights: Iran hold Belgium to stalemateBelgium (BEL) vs Iran (IRN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: (BEL 0-0 IRN), Iran frustrated Belgium for 90 minutes and were rewarded with a valuable point in Group G. Alireza Beiranvand starred with a string of crucial saves, while Nathan Ngoy's red card compounded a frustrating evening for the Red Devils.

Ben Stokes returns as captain after England refuse to blame him for nightclub rowBen Stokes is back at the helm after the ECB concluded its investigation into the nightclub incident that sidelined him for the second Test. While England's captain was sanctioned for breaching team rules, the board cleared him of any involvement in the violence.

9,230 and out: Virat Kohli firmly rejects talks of reversing Test retirementFormer India captain Virat Kohli has firmly dismissed a return to Test cricket, jokingly stating he would rather undersell his new signature shoe than chase more runs to increase its tribute price tag.

West Indies edge closer to semis after bowlers run riot against Sri LankaWomen's T20 World Cup, SL vs WI: West Indies have continued their impressive run in the tournament, beating Sri Lanka by five wickets in Bristol and strengthening their chances of qualifying for the semis.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revenge-arc destroys Sri Lanka in tri-series finalIndia A routed Sri Lanka A in the final of the tri-nation series. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yash Thakur starred with the bat and ball respectively as India hammered Sahan Arachchige's side in Dambulla.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
